title: 1:1 – Seren Voss & Bex Andersen (2026-08-22)
compiled_truth:
Weekly 1:1 between [Seren Voss](people/seren-voss) (CTO) and [Bex Andersen](people/bex-andersen) (senior systems engineer) held Friday afternoon, August 22, 2026. Seren opened by checking in on Bex's workload after a stretch of late nights on the deterministic pipeline refactor. Bex flagged that the refactor is about 80% complete but that she's been blocked on an edge case in the single-pass authentication path — specifically around how the codec handles malformed headers during high-throughput ingest. She described a workaround she'd prototyped but noted it introduced non-determinism in a narrow window, which defeats the whole point.

Seren agreed the workaround was a non-starter and suggested pulling in someone from the verification team to pair on the problem early next week. They discussed whether the issue warranted pushing back the internal milestone (originally set for September 5th). Bex thought they could still hit the date if the pairing session went well, but warned she didn't want to commit to that publicly. Seren said she'd handle expectations with the rest of leadership at [Anchor Codec](companies/anchor) and give Bex room to focus.

Conversation shifted to team dynamics. Bex mentioned that a newer hire on the infrastructure team has been struggling with the codebase and could use better onboarding documentation. She offered to write up a "newcomer's guide" to the codec internals if she could get a half-day carved out next sprint. Seren was enthusiastic about this — said it's the kind of thing that compounds and that she'd prioritize it.

They briefly touched on the upcoming offsite planned for late September. Bex asked whether there'd be a technical deep-dive track or if it was mostly strategy and culture. Seren said she was pushing for at least one session on the single-pass primitives architecture so the whole team could build shared context. Bex volunered to present if needed.

Finally, Seren brought up career growth. She told Bex she's been impressed with her technical leadership over the past few months and wants to discuss a path toward a staff engineer role before year-end. Bex seemed surprised but receptive. They agreed to revisit the topic in their next 1:1 with a rough framework for what staff-level expectations look like at Anchor.
timeline: - **2026-07-10** | [Bex Andersen](people/bex-andersen) begins deterministic pipeline refactor at [Anchor Codec](companies/anchor).
- **2026-08-15** | Bex surfaces edge case in single-pass authentication path during high-throughput testing.
- **2026-08-22** | This 1:1 held. [Seren Voss](people/seren-voss) and Bex discuss refactor blockers, team onboarding gaps, and career pathing.
- **2026-08-26** | Pairing session between Bex and verification team member scheduled to resolve malformed-header issue.
- **2026-09-02** | Bex delivers draft of "newcomer's guide" to codec internals for internal wiki.
- **2026-09-05** | Internal milestone for pipeline refactor — Bex ships on time after pairing session unblocks the issue.
- **2026-09-28** | Anchor offsite; Bex presents deep-dive session on single-pass primitives architecture.
- **2026-11-14** | [Seren Voss](people/seren-voss) formally proposes staff engineer promotion for Bex to leadership team.
- **2026-12-03** | Bex Andersen promoted to Staff Systems Engineer at [Anchor Codec](companies/anchor).
